name: Le Piane (Christoph Kunzli)
visitPolicy: by appointment via email or phone
blurb: Christoph Kunzli is a Swiss wine merchant from Interlaken who tasted Antonio Cerri's 50-year-old Boca at a wine fair in the 1990s and subsequently bought Cerri's farm when the old man died in 1997; he was hooked by a wine he had never heard of from an appellation that had almost ceased to exist. Boca sits on volcanic basalt above Lake Maggiore, producing a style of Nebbiolo with more floral delicacy and mineral snap than its Langhe equivalents. Because production is small and the terroir is genuinely obscure, a visit here is the kind of find that rewards doing the research.
